questions.question: Which OOP principle involves hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features?
questions.explanation: Abstraction hides complex implementation details and shows only the essential features. It's achieved using interfaces or abstract classes to define 'what' something does, not 'how'.

questions.question: In Kotlin, classes are _____ by default and require the _____ keyword to allow inheritance.
questions.explanation: Kotlin classes are 'final' by default — they cannot be extended. You must explicitly mark a class as 'open' to allow other classes to inherit from it.

questions.question: What is Polymorphism?
options: The ability of different objects to respond in their own way to the same method call
Creating multiple instances of the same class
questions.explanation: Polymorphism means 'many forms' — it allows different objects to respond to the same method call in their own way, via method overriding (runtime) or overloading (compile-time).

questions.question: What does Encapsulation protect?
options: An object's internal state from external access
questions.explanation: Encapsulation protects an object's internal state by making variables private and exposing them only through controlled public methods (getters/setters).

questions.question: What does 'Favor Composition over Inheritance' mean?
options: Use object instances to get functionality instead of extending parent classes
questions.explanation: It means building functionality by combining (composing) objects rather than relying on class inheritance hierarchies, which can become rigid and fragile.

questions.question: What is the difference between an Abstract Class and an Interface in Kotlin?
options: A class can implement multiple interfaces but extend only one abstract class
Interfaces can store state but abstract classes cannot
questions.explanation: A class can implement multiple interfaces but can only inherit from one abstract class. Interfaces cannot store state (backing fields), while abstract classes can.

questions.question: Which type of Polymorphism is achieved through method overriding?
questions.explanation: Runtime (or dynamic) polymorphism is achieved through method overriding — the child class provides a specific implementation of a method defined in its parent class.

questions.question: In Kotlin, which keyword is used to implement an interface?
questions.explanation: In Kotlin, both class inheritance and interface implementation use the colon syntax (e.g., 'class Dog : Animal, Runnable'). There are no 'extends' or 'implements' keywords.

questions.question: What is method overloading?
options: Replacing a parent method in a child class
Having multiple functions with the same name but different parameters
questions.explanation: Method overloading (compile-time polymorphism) is having multiple functions with the same name but different parameter types or counts within the same class.

questions.question: Which OOP principle promotes code reuse through parent-child class relationships?
questions.explanation: Inheritance allows a new class to derive properties and behaviors from an existing class. The child class inherits non-private members of the parent, promoting code reuse.
